products.name: Internal Developer Platform
products.description: The front door to platform engineering: a productized self-service layer for service discovery, onboarding, templates, workflow entry points, and developer experience feedback.

interfaces.name: Developer Portal
interfaces.description: Primary self-service workspace for templates, service catalog, platform docs, ownership, and common workflows.

interfaces.name: Platform Self-Service API
interfaces.description: Automation surface for environment creation, template actions, service metadata, and platform workflow triggers.

interfaces.name: Developer Platform CLI
interfaces.description: Local command-line experience for scaffolding, environment actions, diagnostics, and supportable paved-road usage.

neededCapabilities.capabilityName: IDE Workspace and Local Developer Tooling
neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Makes the platform usable inside daily engineering workflows rather than only through portals and backend automation.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Extension packs, dev container definitions, and local workflow helpers
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate local workflow assets against reference services and publish through the developer-tooling release path.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Extends the platform into everyday coding, navigation, and workflow automation tasks while preserving approved boundaries.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Assistant integrations, prompt guardrails, and bounded workflow actions
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate policy boundaries and publish assistant capabilities through the developer-tooling pipeline.
deliveryPaths.target: IDE assistants and internal agent services

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Acts as the front door to the platform and creates shared visibility into services, owners, docs, and dependencies.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Portal UI bundles and static assets
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Build, test, and deploy the portal through the frontend delivery pipeline.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Portal APIs, metadata services, and workflow adapters
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Build, test, scan, and promote the portal services through the standard CI/CD path.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Turns platform standards into a practical starting point for the most common service types and delivery patterns.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Service templates, policy-backed defaults, and scaffolding actions
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate templates, run smoke tests, and publish through the template release workflow.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Connects onboarding, ownership, and workflow automation to the actual repository and pull-request lifecycle engineers work in.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Repository policies, review workflows, ownership metadata, and webhook integrations
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate repository standards and publish workflow integrations through the source-control automation path.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Provides the declarative service model that keeps templates, orchestration, and platform workflows aligned.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Schemas, validators, and supported application-definition bundles
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate app definitions and publish schema updates through the platform workflow pipeline.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Measures whether the internal platform actually reduces friction, cycle time, and cognitive load.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: DevEx data models, telemetry jobs, and platform dashboards
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate models and publish insights through the analytics delivery workflow.

jobsToBeDone.name: Onboard teams onto the standard platform path
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Guide a product team from discovery and service registration to a supportable default setup.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Teams can find the supported path quickly and complete most onboarding steps without bespoke help.

steps.description: Find templates, ownership rules, documentation, and supported service types.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Developer Portal and Service Catalog
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Makes templates, docs, owners, and workflows visible in one place.

steps.description: Generate a service or workflow that already includes the recommended patterns.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Golden Path Templates
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides supported starting points instead of blank setups.

top.name: Platform adoption
top.description: Measures whether teams use the IDP as their normal entry point.

branches.name: Onboarding success
branches.description: Shows whether teams complete core self-service workflows.

children.name: Median team onboarding time
children.description: Elapsed time from first visit to usable platform setup.

children.name: Self-service completion rate
children.description: Share of common onboarding workflows completed without manual help.

top.name: IDP leverage
top.description: Measures whether the IDP changes behavior at organizational scale.

branches.name: Adoption leverage
branches.description: Shows whether mainstream teams move onto the platform.

children.name: Platform adoption across product teams
children.description: Share of product teams using the IDP as the primary path.

children.name: Platform support tickets per onboarding
children.description: Manual support effort still required during onboarding.

productStrategy.vision: Make the IDP the obvious default interface between product teams and the broader platform portfolio.

1_year.focus: Make onboarding and service discovery visibly easier than bespoke setup.

customerKPI.northStar: Median team onboarding time under 2 days

revenueStreams: Lower platform support tickets per onboarding

milestones: Portal becomes the default entry point
Core golden paths are one-click discoverable

3_year.focus: Run the IDP as a mature internal product with explicit service levels and lifecycle states.

customerKPI.northStar: Developer satisfaction with platform experience above 8/10
supporting: Fast discovery of owners and dependencies

businessKPI.northStar: Support load does not scale linearly with adoption

milestones: Lifecycle and support states are explicit
Product research cadence is routine

5_year.focus: Use the IDP as the shared experience layer across a broader internal platform portfolio.

customerKPI.northStar: Most mainstream engineering workflows start through the IDP
supporting: Low cognitive load across the estate

businessKPI.northStar: Platform portfolio decisions use shared adoption evidence

milestones: IDP spans platform portfolio boundaries
Legacy entry points are retired

products.name: Runtime and Delivery Foundation
products.description: Shared runtime, provisioning, rollout, and connectivity foundations that let product teams ship through a supported path instead of custom infrastructure work.

interfaces.name: Runtime Provisioning API
interfaces.description: Platform API for environment creation, workload rollout, and runtime configuration.

interfaces.name: CI/CD Pipeline Library
interfaces.description: Reusable build, test, deploy, and rollback workflows integrated into product-team delivery pipelines.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Creates a standard path for storing and promoting deployable artifacts between CI, CD, and runtime targets.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Registry namespaces, permissions, replication rules, and artifact-promotion workflows
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate artifact policies and publish through the supply-chain release workflow.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Translates supported workload definitions and environment policies into deployable plans with approved resource bindings.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Binding rules, workload graphs, and deployment-plan resolvers
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate orchestration rules and promote the control-plane logic through the platform pipeline.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Creates consistent runtime environments and reduces ticket-based setup work.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Provisioning modules, environment definitions, and policy-backed runtime configs
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate infrastructure code, scan policies, and promote environment bundles through the platform pipeline.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Provides a standard path for quality gates, deployment, promotion, rollback, and release evidence.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Reusable pipeline actions, runners, and release policies
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Test pipeline changes against reference services and publish to the shared automation registry.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Separates build approval from environment promotion so delivery control is explicit after CI completes.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Promotion rules, release-state workflows, and environment synchronization logic
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate rollout behavior and publish promotion workflows through the delivery-control pipeline.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Keeps infrastructure change, state, and drift handling explicit instead of scattered across ad hoc provisioning scripts.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: IaC workflow engines, state backends, and policy-aware apply workflows
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate plans, state transitions, and governance checks through the infrastructure-control pipeline.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Provides approved patterns for service discovery, messaging, network access, and dependency integration.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Connectivity modules, policy defaults, and reference integrations
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate connectivity rules and promote runtime bundles across environments.

jobsToBeDone.name: Ship through a supported runtime path
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Provision environments, deploy safely, and integrate with dependencies through reusable platform patterns.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Teams reach production faster with less local delivery maintenance and fewer environmental surprises.

steps.description: Create preview, test, and production-ready environments without tickets.
Build, test, promote, and roll back through reusable delivery automation.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Delivery Pipelines and Release Automation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides common release automation and evidence.

top.name: Delivery flow
top.description: Measures whether the runtime foundation accelerates software delivery.

branches.name: Setup speed
branches.description: Measures how quickly teams can reach a supported runtime path.

children.name: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
children.description: Elapsed time to first successful supported production deployment.

children.name: Environment provisioning wait time
children.description: Time teams wait for supported runtime environments.

top.name: Runtime leverage
top.description: Measures whether common runtime and delivery patterns replace custom local work.

branches.name: Standardization
branches.description: Shows whether common runtime and delivery logic is reused.

children.name: Lead time for change
children.description: Elapsed time from commit to production on the supported path.

children.name: Bespoke pipeline maintenance effort
children.description: Local maintenance effort avoided through shared delivery automation.

productStrategy.vision: Make safe runtime and delivery defaults so easy to consume that product teams stop rebuilding the same infrastructure patterns locally.

1_year.focus: Eliminate the highest-friction provisioning and deployment bottlenecks.

customerKPI.northStar: Time from repo creation to first production deployment under 1 week
supporting: Environment provisioning wait time under 4 hours

businessKPI.northStar: Lead time for change improves across adopting teams

milestones: Preview and shared environments are self-service
Rollback and promotion paths are reusable

3_year.focus: Make runtime contracts stable enough for specialist platform reuse.

customerKPI.northStar: Architectural rework from platform changes materially reduced

businessKPI.northStar: More teams use shared runtime patterns

milestones: Runtime patterns are versioned
Dependency contracts are explicit

5_year.focus: Run the runtime layer as a durable foundation shared across the platform portfolio.

customerKPI.northStar: Most mainstream workloads use the supported runtime path

businessKPI.northStar: Common foundation spend displaces duplicate local foundation work

milestones: Runtime layer is portfolio-wide
Legacy bespoke delivery paths are isolated or retired

products.name: Secure Software Supply Chain
products.description: Identity, policy, artifact-trust, and compliance-control product that makes the standard path safer and more auditable without pushing teams into manual governance queues.

interfaces.name: Policy and Identity Control API
interfaces.description: Platform surfaces for identity, secret delivery, artifact trust, and compliance checks.

interfaces.name: Security and Compliance Posture Dashboard
interfaces.description: Visibility into control coverage, exception state, and supply-chain posture across services.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Makes source-level engineering quality part of the standard path rather than a repository-by-repository add-on.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Quality policies, findings aggregation, and CI-integrated source analysis workflows
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate rule packs and publish source-analysis integrations through the quality-control path.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Provides secure workload identity, secret distribution, and access standards used across the estate.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Identity policies, secret-delivery integrations, and access workflows
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate policies and rollout changes through controlled security automation.

neededCapabilities.capabilityName: Policy as Code and Compliance Controls
neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Moves engineering controls into templates, pipelines, and runtime enforcement rather than manual reviews.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Policy bundles, evidence collectors, and exception workflows
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Test policy bundles against reference services and publish through the control-plane pipeline.

neededCapabilities.capabilityName: Artifact Supply Chain and Base Images
neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Creates trusted, reusable software delivery components with provenance, scanning, and update discipline.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Base images, package policies, provenance attestations, and registry controls
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Build, sign, scan, and publish trusted artifacts through the supply-chain pipeline.

deliveryPaths.whyNeeded: Adds runtime and environment posture visibility beyond build-time controls alone.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Posture collectors, runtime findings, and security-operations dashboards
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate posture signals and publish protections through the security-operations release path.

jobsToBeDone.name: Make the default delivery path safe by design
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Embed identity, policy, and trusted artifacts into the normal workflow so teams comply by default.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Control coverage rises without central review becoming the bottleneck.

steps.step: Apply secure identity and secret defaults
steps.description: Standardize sensitive runtime access and secret handling.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity, Secrets and Access Guardrails
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides workload identity and least-privilege patterns.

steps.description: Validate compliance and trusted artifacts as part of delivery automation.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Policy as Code and Compliance Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns control checks and exceptions into reusable workflows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Artifact Supply Chain and Base Images

top.name: Inherited controls
top.description: Measures whether teams get strong baseline controls by using the standard path.

branches.name: Coverage
branches.description: Shows whether required controls are broadly inherited.

children.name: Production services with baseline controls
children.description: Share of production services inheriting required identity, policy, and artifact controls.

children.name: Production services with standard telemetry
children.description: Share of production services with the standard telemetry baseline active.

top.name: Governance leverage
top.description: Measures whether controls reduce risk without increasing central manual effort.

branches.name: Control economics
branches.description: Shows whether automation replaces manual governance work.

children.name: Manual governance workload
children.description: Time spent on repetitive central governance and policy review tasks.

children.name: Change failure rate
children.description: Failures linked to weak baselines or unsafe delivery paths.

productStrategy.vision: Turn security and compliance from a late review gate into a productized control plane built into the everyday engineering workflow.

1_year.focus: Push baseline controls into templates, runtime setup, and delivery automation.

customerKPI.northStar: Production services with baseline controls above 90%
supporting: Production services with standard telemetry above 90%

revenueStreams: Lower change failure rate from baseline issues

milestones: Trusted artifacts become the standard path
Exception states are explicit and time-bound

3_year.focus: Make evidence and exception handling product-grade and portfolio-aware.

customerKPI.northStar: Exception review cycle time under 5 business days

businessKPI.northStar: Major incidents from preventable platform variance materially reduced

milestones: Control evidence is continuous
Most high-frequency exceptions are productized

5_year.focus: Use the control plane as a shared governance backbone across the platform portfolio.

supporting: Low-friction compliance on the standard path

businessKPI.northStar: Governance scales without linear headcount growth

milestones: Control-plane contracts are shared portfolio-wide
Legacy review-heavy paths are minimized

products.name: Reliability and Engineering Insights
products.description: Shared telemetry, incident automation, and engineering-insight product that helps platform and product teams operate services and govern platform investment with evidence.

interfaces.name: Platform Operations Dashboard
interfaces.description: Operational visibility into service health, incidents, usage, cost, and platform adoption.

interfaces.name: Incident and Telemetry Event Streams
interfaces.description: Event surfaces used to route alerts, incidents, ownership changes, and automation actions.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Provides the common signal model required for production operations, debugging, and engineering visibility.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Log, metric, trace, and dashboard defaults plus collection pipelines
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate collectors and publish observability bundles through the shared platform pipeline.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Standardizes response, escalation, post-incident action management, and rollback coordination.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Runbooks, alert routes, incident workflows, and remediation automations
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Test incident paths in simulation and publish to the operational control plane.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Helps product and platform teams see the operational and economic consequences of platform choices.

deliveryPaths.deploymentUnit: Usage models, cost-attribution jobs, and platform-value dashboards
deliveryPaths.pipeline: Validate attribution models and publish through the engineering insights pipeline.

jobsToBeDone.name: See platform reliability and value clearly enough to act
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Provide shared telemetry, incident workflows, and value reporting that guide operational response and platform investment decisions.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Teams respond faster to shared failures and leadership can govern the platform portfolio on evidence.

steps.description: Standardize logs, metrics, traces, and usage signals across the platform estate.
Route incidents and publish platform-value insights that change operational and portfolio decisions.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cost, Capacity and Usage Insights
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects reliability and usage data to platform economics.

top.name: Operational clarity
top.description: Measures whether teams can see and respond to shared-platform behavior quickly.

branches.name: Incident response
branches.description: Measures speed and quality of response to shared failures.

children.name: Incident MTTR for common platform failure classes
children.description: Mean time to recover from recurring shared-platform failures.

children.name: Production services with standard telemetry
children.description: Share of production services reporting the standard telemetry baseline.

top.name: Platform decision quality
top.description: Measures whether leaders can govern the platform using joined operational and economic evidence.

branches.name: Evidence quality
branches.description: Shows whether platform value and cost are visible enough to guide investment.

children.name: Trusted weekly platform value pack
children.description: Regular joined reporting across adoption, flow, reliability, and cost.

children.name: Engineering cost-of-delay and idle-environment waste visible weekly across the platform estate
children.description: Visibility into avoidable engineering and environment waste.

productStrategy.vision: Make operational and economic reality of the platform visible enough that teams can improve reliability fast and leadership can invest in the platform portfolio intelligently.

1_year.focus: Raise telemetry consistency and incident response quality across the shared estate.

customerKPI.northStar: Incident MTTR for common platform failure classes under 30 minutes
supporting: Production services with standard telemetry above 90%

businessKPI.northStar: Trusted weekly platform value pack in regular leadership use

milestones: Alert quality improves
Shared operational dashboard becomes trusted

3_year.focus: Join cost, adoption, and reliability evidence into one operating view.

customerKPI.northStar: Platform value data trusted by product and engineering leadership
supporting: Shared operational metrics across platform products

businessKPI.northStar: Platform portfolio prioritization improves through shared evidence
revenueStreams: More disciplined investment and retirement decisions

milestones: Value pack becomes portfolio review input
Usage and cost are tied to product decisions

5_year.focus: Make shared reliability and value insight a durable control surface for the platform portfolio.

customerKPI.northStar: Operational and economic blind spots are materially reduced across the platform estate

businessKPI.northStar: Platform governance runs on evidence, not fragmented reports
revenueStreams: Higher leverage from central platform investment

milestones: Insight model spans the full platform portfolio
Operational and value reporting are tightly linked
